description: Intel 8086/8088 baseline instruction set (16-bit real mode). All instructions in this file were introduced with the original Intel 8086 processor and are available in 16-bit, 32-bit, and 64-bit modes unless otherwise noted.

opcodes.description: Move byte at seg:offset to AL
Move word at seg:offset to AX
Move imm8 to byte register (short encoding)
Move imm16 to word register (short encoding)

instructions.description: Copies a byte or word from source to destination. The most fundamental data transfer instruction.
Decrements SP by 2 and stores a 16-bit value on the stack.
Loads a 16-bit value from the top of the stack into the destination, then increments SP by 2.

opcodes.description: Exchange AX with word register (short encoding; 0x90=NOP)

instructions.description: Swaps the contents of two registers or a register and a memory location atomically.

opcodes.description: Set AL = [BX + AL] — byte table lookup

instructions.description: Translates a byte in AL using a lookup table pointed to by BX. Sets AL to the value at [BX+AL].

instructions.sasm_phrase: read byte from <port> to al / read word from <port> to ax

opcodes.description: Read byte from I/O port (imm8 address) into AL
Read word from I/O port (imm8 address) into AX
Read byte from I/O port (DX address) into AL
Read word from I/O port (DX address) into AX

instructions.description: Reads a byte or word from an I/O port into AL or AX.
instructions.notes: Ring-0 / privileged in protected and long mode.

instructions.sasm_phrase: write byte from al to <port> / write word from ax to <port>

opcodes.description: Write byte from AL to I/O port (imm8 address)
Write word from AX to I/O port (imm8 address)
Write byte from AL to I/O port (DX address)
Write word from AX to I/O port (DX address)

instructions.description: Writes a byte or word from AL or AX to an I/O port.
instructions.notes: Ring-0 / privileged in protected and long mode.

opcodes.description: Load effective address of mem into 16-bit register (no memory read)

instructions.description: Loads the effective address of a memory operand into a register (no memory access performed).

opcodes.description: Load far pointer: DS = mem[2], reg = mem[0]

instructions.description: Loads a far pointer from memory into DS:reg (DS gets the segment, reg gets the offset).
instructions.notes: Removed in 64-bit mode; opcode 0xC5 repurposed as VEX prefix.

opcodes.description: Load far pointer: ES = mem[2], reg = mem[0]

instructions.description: Loads a far pointer from memory into ES:reg (ES gets the segment, reg gets the offset).
instructions.notes: Removed in 64-bit mode; opcode 0xC4 repurposed as VEX prefix.

opcodes.description: Load AH from lower byte of FLAGS (SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF)

instructions.description: Copies the low byte of the FLAGS register (SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF) into AH.

opcodes.description: Store AH into lower byte of FLAGS (SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF)

instructions.description: Copies AH into the low byte of the FLAGS register, setting SF, ZF, AF, PF, and CF.

opcodes.description: Push FLAGS (16-bit) register onto stack; 32-bit: PUSHFD (EFLAGS); 64-bit: PUSHFQ (RFLAGS)

instructions.description: Pushes the FLAGS (16-bit) register onto the stack.
instructions.notes: In 32-bit mode: PUSHFD. In 64-bit mode: PUSHFQ. All share opcode 0x9C.

opcodes.description: Pop FLAGS (16-bit) register from stack; 32-bit: POPFD (EFLAGS); 64-bit: POPFQ (RFLAGS)

instructions.description: Pops a 16-bit value from the stack into the FLAGS register.
instructions.notes: In 32-bit mode: POPFD. In 64-bit mode: POPFQ. All share opcode 0x9D.

instructions.description: Adds src to dst; result stored in dst. Updates CF, OF, ZF, SF, PF, AF.

opcodes.description: Add sign-extended imm8 + CF to r/m16

instructions.description: Adds src plus the Carry Flag to dst. Used for multi-precision addition.
Subtracts src from dst; result stored in dst. Updates CF, OF, ZF, SF, PF, AF.

opcodes.description: Subtract sign-extended imm8 + CF from r/m16

instructions.description: Subtracts src and the Carry Flag from dst. Used for multi-precision subtraction.

opcodes.description: Increment 16-bit register (short encoding; bytes 0x40-0x47 are REX prefixes in 64-bit mode)

instructions.description: Increments dst by 1. Does not affect CF.
instructions.notes: Short register forms 0x40–0x47 are REX prefix bytes in 64-bit mode; use 0xFF /0 there.

opcodes.description: Decrement 16-bit register (short encoding; bytes 0x48-0x4F are REX prefixes in 64-bit mode)

instructions.description: Decrements dst by 1. Does not affect CF.
instructions.notes: Short register forms 0x48–0x4F are REX prefix bytes in 64-bit mode; use 0xFF /1 there.

instructions.description: Unsigned multiply: AX = AL × src (byte) or DX:AX = AX × src (word).
Signed multiply. In the 8086, AX = AL × src or DX:AX = AX × src.
instructions.notes: Two-operand (0x0F 0xAF) and three-operand (0x69/0x6B) forms added in 80386.

opcodes.description: Unsigned: AL = AX ÷ r/m8; AH = remainder
Unsigned: AX = DX:AX ÷ r/m16; DX = remainder

instructions.description: Unsigned divide: AL = AX ÷ src, AH = remainder (byte); or AX = DX:AX ÷ src, DX = remainder (word).

opcodes.description: Signed: AL = AX ÷ r/m8; AH = remainder
Signed: AX = DX:AX ÷ r/m16; DX = remainder

instructions.description: Signed divide; same structure as DIV but for signed integers.
Negates dst (two's complement: dst = 0 − dst).

opcodes.description: Compare r8 with r/m8 (set flags for r/m8 - r8)
Compare r/m8 with r8 (set flags for r8 - r/m8)

instructions.description: Subtracts op2 from op1 and discards the result, only updating FLAGS. Used before conditional jumps.

opcodes.description: Sign-extend AL into AX (16-bit); CWDE in 32-bit (AX→EAX); CDQE in 64-bit (EAX→RAX)

instructions.description: Sign-extends AL into AX (byte→word).
instructions.notes: Same opcode 0x98. In 32-bit mode: CWDE (sign-extends AX→EAX). In 64-bit mode with REX.W: CDQE (sign-extends EAX→RAX).

opcodes.description: Sign-extend AX into DX:AX (16-bit); CDQ in 32-bit (EAX→EDX:EAX); CQO in 64-bit (RAX→RDX:RAX)

instructions.description: Sign-extends AX into DX:AX (word→doubleword). Used before IDIV.
instructions.notes: Same opcode 0x99. In 32-bit mode: CDQ (EAX→EDX:EAX). In 64-bit mode with REX.W: CQO (RAX→RDX:RAX).

opcodes.description: Decimal adjust AL after BCD addition

instructions.description: Decimal Adjust AL after Addition. Converts AL to packed BCD format after an ADD/ADC.
instructions.notes: Opcode 0x27 conflicts with REX prefix space in 64-bit mode.

opcodes.description: Decimal adjust AL after BCD subtraction

instructions.description: Decimal Adjust AL after Subtraction. Converts AL to packed BCD format after a SUB/SBB.

opcodes.description: ASCII adjust AL after unpacked BCD addition

instructions.description: ASCII Adjust AL after Addition. Adjusts AL for unpacked BCD addition.

opcodes.description: ASCII adjust AL after unpacked BCD subtraction

instructions.description: ASCII Adjust AL after Subtraction. Adjusts AL for unpacked BCD subtraction.

opcodes.description: ASCII adjust AX after unpacked BCD multiply (imm8=0x0A for decimal)

instructions.description: ASCII Adjust AX after Multiplication. Converts AX to unpacked BCD after a MUL byte instruction.

opcodes.description: ASCII adjust AX before unpacked BCD divide (imm8=0x0A for decimal)

instructions.description: ASCII Adjust AX before Division. Converts two unpacked BCD digits in AH:AL to binary in AL before a DIV.
Bitwise AND of dst and src; result in dst. Clears OF and CF.
Bitwise OR of dst and src; result in dst. Clears OF and CF.
Bitwise XOR of dst and src; result in dst. Commonly used to zero a register (XOR AX, AX).
Bitwise complement of dst (one's complement). Does not affect FLAGS.

opcodes.description: AND r8 with r/m8; set ZF, SF, PF; discard result
AND r16 with r/m16; set flags; discard result
AND imm8 with AL; set flags
AND imm16 with AX; set flags
AND imm8 with r/m8; set flags
AND imm16 with r/m16; set flags

instructions.description: Bitwise AND of op1 and op2; discards the result, only updating ZF, SF, PF. Used before conditional jumps.
Logical/arithmetic left shift; zeros fill LSB; MSB → CF.
instructions.notes: Immediate-count forms 0xC0/0xC1 require 80186 or later.

instructions.description: Logical right shift; zeros fill MSB; LSB → CF.

opcodes.description: Arithmetic shift r/m8 right by 1 (sign preserved)
Arithmetic shift r/m16 right by 1 (sign preserved)
Arithmetic shift r/m8 right by CL
Arithmetic shift r/m16 right by CL
Arithmetic shift r/m8 right by imm8 (80186+)
Arithmetic shift r/m16 right by imm8 (80186+)

instructions.description: Arithmetic right shift; sign bit preserved (MSB replicated).

opcodes.description: Rotate r/m8 left by 1; MSB wraps to LSB and CF

instructions.description: Rotate bits left; MSB wraps to LSB and CF.

opcodes.description: Rotate r/m8 right by 1; LSB wraps to MSB and CF

instructions.description: Rotate bits right; LSB wraps to MSB and CF.

opcodes.description: Rotate r/m8 left through CF by 1
Rotate r/m16 left through CF by 1
Rotate r/m8 left through CF by CL
Rotate r/m16 left through CF by CL
Rotate r/m8 left through CF by imm8 (80186+)
Rotate r/m16 left through CF by imm8 (80186+)

instructions.description: Rotate left through Carry: like ROL but uses CF as an extra bit in the rotation.

opcodes.description: Rotate r/m8 right through CF by 1
Rotate r/m16 right through CF by 1
Rotate r/m8 right through CF by CL
Rotate r/m16 right through CF by CL
Rotate r/m8 right through CF by imm8 (80186+)
Rotate r/m16 right through CF by imm8 (80186+)

instructions.description: Rotate right through Carry: like ROR but uses CF as an extra bit in the rotation.
Repeat prefix: repeats the following string instruction CX times, decrementing CX each iteration.

opcodes.description: Repeat string operation while CX!=0 and ZF=1; same prefix byte as REP

instructions.description: Repeat while equal/zero: repeats while CX ≠ 0 and ZF = 1. Used with CMPS and SCAS.
instructions.notes: Shares opcode 0xF3 with REP; semantics differ for CMPS/SCAS vs. MOVS/STOS.

opcodes.description: Repeat string operation while CX!=0 and ZF=0

instructions.description: Repeat while not equal/not zero: repeats while CX ≠ 0 and ZF = 0. Used with CMPS and SCAS.

instructions.sasm_phrase: copy string byte / copy string word / copy string

opcodes.description: Copy byte from [DS:SI] to [ES:DI]; advance SI and DI
Copy word from [DS:SI] to [ES:DI]; advance SI and DI by 2

instructions.description: Copies a byte or word from [DS:SI] to [ES:DI] and updates SI and DI (direction determined by DF).
instructions.notes: 0xA5 also encodes MOVSD (dword) in 32-bit mode and MOVSQ (qword) in 64-bit mode with REX.W.

instructions.sasm_phrase: compare strings byte / compare strings word / compare strings

opcodes.description: Compare byte at [DS:SI] with [ES:DI]; advance SI and DI
Compare word at [DS:SI] with [ES:DI]; advance SI and DI by 2

instructions.description: Compares a byte or word at [DS:SI] with [ES:DI] and updates SI and DI. Sets FLAGS without storing a result.
instructions.notes: 0xA7 also encodes CMPSD (dword) in 32-bit mode and CMPSQ (qword) in 64-bit mode with REX.W.

instructions.sasm_phrase: scan string byte / scan string word / scan string

opcodes.description: Scan: compare AL with byte at [ES:DI]; advance DI
Scan: compare AX with word at [ES:DI]; advance DI by 2

instructions.description: Scans a byte or word at [ES:DI] against AL or AX; updates DI. Used to search memory for a value.
instructions.notes: 0xAF also encodes SCASD (dword) in 32-bit mode and SCASQ (qword) in 64-bit mode with REX.W.

instructions.sasm_phrase: load string byte / load string word / load string

opcodes.description: Load byte from [DS:SI] into AL; advance SI
Load word from [DS:SI] into AX; advance SI by 2

instructions.description: Loads a byte or word from [DS:SI] into AL or AX and updates SI.
instructions.notes: 0xAD also encodes LODSD (dword) in 32-bit mode and LODSQ (qword) in 64-bit mode with REX.W.

instructions.sasm_phrase: store string byte / store string word / store string

opcodes.description: Store AL into byte at [ES:DI]; advance DI
Store AX into word at [ES:DI]; advance DI by 2

instructions.description: Stores AL or AX into [ES:DI] and updates DI. Used with REP to fill a memory block.
instructions.notes: 0xAB also encodes STOSD (dword) in 32-bit mode and STOSQ (qword) in 64-bit mode with REX.W.

opcodes.description: Far jump (seg:off) — not valid in 64-bit mode
Far indirect jump through memory — not valid in 64-bit mode

instructions.description: Unconditional jump to the target address (short, near, or far).
instructions.notes: Far forms 0xEA are not encodable in 64-bit long mode.

opcodes.description: Near call: push IP, IP += sign-extended imm16
Far call (seg:off) — not valid in 64-bit mode

instructions.description: Pushes the return address (IP or CS:IP) onto the stack and jumps to the target (near or far call).
instructions.notes: Far call form 0x9A is not valid in 64-bit long mode.

opcodes.description: Near return: pop IP from stack
Near return and pop imm16 bytes from stack

instructions.description: Returns from a near call: pops IP from the stack. Optional immediate pops additional bytes from the stack.

opcodes.description: Far return: pop IP then CS from stack
Far return and pop imm16 bytes

instructions.description: Returns from a far call: pops IP then CS from the stack.
instructions.notes: Rarely used in modern flat-memory OSes; segmentation is largely unused.

instructions.description: Jump if Equal / Zero (ZF = 1).

instructions.sasm_phrase: goto <label> if not equal / if not equal { }
instructions.description: Jump if Not Equal / Not Zero (ZF = 0).

opcodes.description: Short jump if above unsigned (CF=0 and ZF=0)

instructions.description: Jump if Above (unsigned greater than): CF = 0 and ZF = 0.

instructions.sasm_phrase: goto <label> if above or equal / if above or equal { }

opcodes.description: Short jump if above or equal unsigned (CF=0)
Near jump if above or equal unsigned

instructions.description: Jump if Above or Equal / No Carry (unsigned ≥): CF = 0.
Jump if Below / Carry (unsigned less than): CF = 1.

instructions.sasm_phrase: goto <label> if below or equal / if below or equal { }

opcodes.description: Short jump if below or equal unsigned (CF=1 or ZF=1)
Near jump if below or equal unsigned

instructions.description: Jump if Below or Equal (unsigned ≤): CF = 1 or ZF = 1.

opcodes.description: Short jump if greater signed (ZF=0 and SF=OF)

instructions.description: Jump if Greater (signed greater than): ZF = 0 and SF = OF.

instructions.sasm_phrase: goto <label> if greater or equal / if greater or equal { }

opcodes.description: Short jump if greater or equal signed (SF=OF)
Near jump if greater or equal signed

instructions.description: Jump if Greater or Equal (signed ≥): SF = OF.
Jump if Less (signed less than): SF ≠ OF.

instructions.sasm_phrase: goto <label> if less or equal / if less or equal { }

opcodes.description: Short jump if less or equal signed (ZF=1 or SF!=OF)
Near jump if less or equal signed

instructions.description: Jump if Less or Equal (signed ≤): ZF = 1 or SF ≠ OF.
Jump if Overflow: OF = 1.

instructions.sasm_phrase: goto <label> if no overflow / if no overflow { }
instructions.description: Jump if No Overflow: OF = 0.
Jump if Sign (negative): SF = 1.
Jump if No Sign (non-negative): SF = 0.

instructions.sasm_phrase: goto <label> if parity even / if parity even { }
instructions.description: Jump if Parity / Parity Even: PF = 1.

instructions.sasm_phrase: goto <label> if parity odd / if parity odd { }
instructions.description: Jump if No Parity / Parity Odd: PF = 0.

opcodes.description: Short jump if CX=0; in 32-bit mode JECXZ (ECX=0); in 64-bit mode JRCXZ (RCX=0)

instructions.description: Jump if CX is Zero (flag-free test). In 32-bit: JECXZ. In 64-bit: JRCXZ.
instructions.notes: Short jump only. Same opcode 0xE3; register tested (CX/ECX/RCX) depends on address-size prefix.

opcodes.description: Decrement CX; short jump if CX != 0

instructions.description: Decrements CX; jumps to the short label if CX ≠ 0.
instructions.notes: Compilers prefer DEC + JNZ for performance on modern CPUs.

opcodes.description: Decrement CX; short jump if CX!=0 and ZF=1

instructions.description: Decrements CX; jumps if CX ≠ 0 and ZF = 1.

instructions.sasm_phrase: repeat cx times while not equal { }

opcodes.description: Decrement CX; short jump if CX!=0 and ZF=0

instructions.description: Decrements CX; jumps if CX ≠ 0 and ZF = 0.

opcodes.description: Software interrupt: push FLAGS, CS, IP; jump to vector n

instructions.description: Software interrupt: pushes FLAGS, CS, and IP, then jumps to the interrupt vector n.
Interrupt on Overflow: generates INT 4 if OF = 1.
instructions.notes: Opcode 0xCE reassigned in 64-bit long mode.

opcodes.description: Interrupt return: pop IP, CS, FLAGS from stack; in 32-bit: IRETD; in 64-bit: IRETQ

instructions.description: Interrupt Return: pops IP, CS, and FLAGS from the stack. Returns from an interrupt handler.
instructions.notes: In 32-bit mode: IRETD. In 64-bit mode with REX.W: IRETQ (pops 40 bytes).

instructions.description: Clear Carry Flag: sets CF = 0.
Set Carry Flag: sets CF = 1.
Complement Carry Flag: inverts CF.

opcodes.description: Clear Direction Flag: DF = 0 (string ops auto-increment SI/DI)

instructions.description: Clear Direction Flag: sets DF = 0 so string operations auto-increment SI/DI.

opcodes.description: Set Direction Flag: DF = 1 (string ops auto-decrement SI/DI)

instructions.description: Set Direction Flag: sets DF = 1 so string operations auto-decrement SI/DI.

opcodes.description: Clear Interrupt Flag: IF = 0 (disable maskable hardware interrupts)

instructions.description: Clear Interrupt Flag: disables maskable hardware interrupts (IF = 0). Requires privilege.
instructions.notes: Requires ring-0 in protected/long mode.

opcodes.description: Set Interrupt Flag: IF = 1 (enable maskable hardware interrupts)

instructions.description: Set Interrupt Flag: enables maskable hardware interrupts (IF = 1). Requires privilege.
instructions.notes: Requires ring-0 in protected/long mode.

opcodes.description: No operation (encoded as XCHG AX, AX on 8086)

instructions.description: No Operation: does nothing for one bus cycle. Often used for padding, timing, or pipeline alignment.
instructions.notes: Multi-byte NOP forms available (0x0F 0x1F + ModRM) from P6 onward for alignment padding.

opcodes.description: Halt: suspend until next interrupt or reset

instructions.description: Halt: stops instruction execution until a hardware interrupt or reset occurs. Requires privilege (ring-0).
instructions.notes: Requires ring-0.

opcodes.description: Wait until FPU not busy (TEST pin asserted)

instructions.description: Wait: suspends CPU execution until the FPU (8087) signals completion via the TEST pin.
instructions.notes: Modern CPUs integrate the FPU; synchronization is handled internally.

opcodes.description: Assert LOCK# bus signal for the following memory-modifying instruction

instructions.description: Prefix that asserts the bus LOCK# signal for the duration of the following memory-modifying instruction, ensuring atomicity.
instructions.notes: Valid with XCHG, ADD, ADC, SUB, SBB, AND, OR, XOR, NOT, NEG, INC, DEC, BTS, BTR, BTC, CMPXCHG, CMPXCHG8B, XADD.
